title: Lesson Quiz: Economic Systems in a Global Context
description: Assessment of the concept that Comparing systems across nations.

choices.text: An economy in which the government makes many major production decisions
choices.feedback: That best describes a command economy.

choices.text: An economy in which individuals and businesses make many economic decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. In a market economy, private individuals and businesses make many key decisions.

choices.text: An economy with no trade at all
choices.feedback: Market economy refers to how decisions are made, not whether trade exists.

choices.text: An economy that combines only foreign and domestic trade
choices.feedback: This does not define a market economy.

questions.prompt: A command economy is one in which the government makes many major economic decisions.

choices.feedback: Correct. In a command economy, the government plays the main decision-making role.
This statement is true because command economies involve major government control of decisions.

choices.text: An economy that avoids all government involvement
choices.feedback: A mixed economy includes some government role rather than avoiding it completely.

choices.text: An economy that combines features of market and command systems
choices.feedback: Correct. A mixed economy includes elements of both market and command systems.

choices.text: An economy where trade happens only within one country
choices.feedback: This does not define a mixed economy.

choices.text: An economy where consumers make no decisions
choices.feedback: Consumers and businesses still make many decisions in mixed economies.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful to compare economic systems across countries?

choices.text: Because all countries organize their economies in exactly the same way
choices.feedback: Countries do not all organize economic decisions in the same way.

choices.text: Because comparison helps explain how different countries make economic decisions and still participate in global trade
choices.feedback: Correct. Comparing systems helps explain how countries organize decisions and operate in the global economy.

choices.text: Because only command economies participate in trade
choices.feedback: Countries with different systems can all participate in trade.

choices.text: Because economic systems never affect production decisions
choices.feedback: Economic systems do affect how production and resource decisions are made.

questions.prompt: Countries with different economic systems can still participate in global trade.

choices.feedback: Correct. Countries with different economic systems can still trade with one another.
This statement is true because countries with different systems can participate in global trade.
